name: Model Weave

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
# Model Weave v0.1.19

Model Weave v0.1.19 adds the Flow Diagram MVP, improves Diagnostics guidance, and makes Color Scheme editing and review easier.

## Highlights

### Flow Diagram MVP

- Added `type: flow_diagram` for communication and data handoff diagrams.
- Supports screen, context, process, store, session, and external objects.
- Uses `Objects.domain` to group nodes into Mermaid subgraphs.
- Applies `target=domain` Color Scheme rows to Flow Diagram domain groups.
- Uses richer Flow semantics with:

  `id | from | to | kind | trigger | data | condition | notes`

- Renders edge labels using trigger / kind / data.
- Adds rich hover interactions for Flow Diagram nodes and edge labels.
- Resolved model/data references open Obsidian Page Preview.
- Unresolved/plain references fall back to Model Weave detail cards or safe tooltips.
- Adds diagnostics for unresolved Wikilinks in `Flows.data` while preserving plain text labels.

### Diagnostics improvements

- Improved schema-driven expected header guidance.
- Shows expected headers only for malformed table-header diagnostics.
- Suppresses cascading row-level diagnostics when table headers are malformed.
- Distinguishes local Flow Diagram endpoints from external model references.
- Treats structured malformed table headers as errors.
- Improves Color Scheme and Source Links diagnostics.
- Validates Wikilinks in `Flows.data` for DFD and Flow Diagram without requiring plain labels to resolve.

### Color Scheme improvements

- Added Color Scheme preview editing support with swatches and native color picker controls.
- Makes `fill`, `stroke`, and `text` easier to edit in Color Scheme previews.
- Compact Applied Color Scheme display now uses:

  `Target | Kind | Preview | Notes | Source`

- Raw color codes remain available through accessibility metadata without duplicate native browser tooltips.

## Notes

- `flow_diagram` is an MVP and currently renders the Internal Detail View.
- Surface View, Communication View, folding/projection, and transition coverage generation are planned for later versions.
- Existing `dfd_diagram` format remains unchanged.
releases.name: Model Weave v0.1.19

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
# Model Weave v0.1.18

This release improves the viewer review workflow, Diagnostics usability, and small manual-edit support for Model Weave Markdown files.

## Highlights

- Fixed hover preview stability near the window top edge and in Focus mode.
- Added stable lower panel tabs for viewer review information.
- Applied lower panel tabs to both graph and non-graph model review views.
- Improved Diagnostics tab layout and action grouping.
- Added bulk Markdown copy actions for diagnostics.
- Added manual-edit guidance to diagnostic details.
- Added Quick Fix MVP for missing frontmatter `id` / `name`.
- Fully empty Markdown table data rows are now ignored as editing noise.
releases.name: Model Weave v0.1.18

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.17

Model Weave 0.1.17 improves App Process Business Flow usability.

This release focuses on making Business Flow easier to view, navigate, and edit while keeping Markdown as the source of truth.

### Highlights

#### App Process Business Flow direction

Business Flow can now be displayed horizontally or vertically.

* Added LR / TD direction support
* Added `flow_direction` frontmatter
* Added default Business Flow direction setting
* Toolbar direction override is supported

#### Expanded App Process step kinds

Business Flow now supports additional `Steps.kind` values and renders them with more appropriate shapes.

Newly supported kinds include:

* `event`
* `api`
* `batch`
* `message`
* `data`
* `store`
* `wait`
* `error`
* `connector`
* `external`

Blank or unknown kinds still fall back to a normal process node.

#### Ref-aware Business Flow step hover/click

Business Flow step nodes now use referenced models as their hover preview and click navigation target when possible.

Targets are resolved from:

* `Steps.screen`
* `Steps.invoke`
* `Steps.rule`
* `Steps.input`
* `Steps.output`
* `Inputs.data`
* `Inputs.source`
* `Outputs.data`
* `Outputs.target`

If no referenced model can be resolved, the step falls back to the app_process source file.

#### Flow Connect Mode

Added Connect Flow Mode for App Process Business Flow previews.

When enabled, users can click two step nodes to add a `from -> to` row to the `## Flows` table.

* First click selects the source step
* Second click selects the target step
* `## Flows` is created when missing
* Duplicate `from -> to` rows are skipped
* The Markdown file is saved and the preview is refreshed

DFD flow editing is not included.

#### Connect Flow UX improvements

* Added active toolbar state for Connect Flow Mode
* Added status text while Connect Flow Mode is active
* Preserved View Only mode during Connect Flow operations
* Normal hover/click navigation is restored when Connect Flow Mode is off

### Notes

Markdown remains the source of truth.
Flow Connect Mode is a visual input helper that updates the underlying `## Flows` table.

### Validation

Checked with:

```text
node --test test/*.test.mjs
node ./node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck
node esbuild.config.mjs production
./node_modules/.bin/eslint "src/**/*.ts"
node scripts/check-css-compat.mjs
git diff --check
```
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.17

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.16

This release improves Viewer / Preview usability, adds hover previews and click navigation for resolved nodes and boxes, and updates the minimum supported Obsidian version.

### Compatibility note

Starting with 0.1.16, Model Weave requires Obsidian 1.8.7 or later.

Existing released versions remain unchanged in `versions.json`:

* `0.1.14`: `1.7.2`
* `0.1.15`: `1.7.2`
* `0.1.16`: `1.8.7`

### Highlights

* Added hover preview and click navigation support for resolved nodes and boxes in supported Model Weave views.
* Added Class / ER Mermaid and Mermaid Detail node interactions.
* Fixed ER Mermaid Detail navigation by supporting Mermaid ER SVG entity IDs and pointerup fallback navigation.
* Improved custom renderer hover previews for Screen, Class, ER, and object context views.
* Improved Mermaid node interactions for Weave Map, DFD, App-Process, and Domain views.
* Scoped preview render mode switching to each preview leaf.
* Updated README and documentation for 0.1.16.
* Fixed release lint warnings and reduced eslint output to warning 0.

### Hover preview limitation

Hover previews rely on Obsidian's hover-link / Page Preview behavior. They may not appear near the top edge of the Obsidian window or while using Model Weave Focus mode.

For resolved nodes and boxes, click navigation is the reliable fallback.

### Verification

Passed:

* `/usr/bin/node --test test/*.test.mjs`
* `/usr/bin/node ./node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck`
* `/usr/bin/node esbuild.config.mjs production`
* `./node_modules/.bin/eslint "src/**/*.ts"`
* `/usr/bin/node scripts/check-css-compat.mjs`
* `git diff --check`

Result:

* 142 tests passed
* TypeScript check passed
* Production build passed
* eslint warning 0
* CSS compatibility check passed
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.16

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.15

### Fixes

- Fixed an Obsidian plugin lint issue in the Weave Map layout implementation.
- Replaced direct `element.style.*` assignments with `setCssStyles(...)`.
- Preserved the existing Weave Map layout behavior and rendering stability.

### Notes

This is a patch release following 0.1.14.  
It does not add new user-facing functionality.

## Summary

Prepare Model Weave 0.1.14 release.

### Highlights

* Added Weave Map to the Impact / Relationship View.
* Added Compact / Full display modes for Weave Map.
* Deduplicated repeated Source Links, unresolved references, and repeated edges.
* Added PNG and PNG↗ buttons to graph toolbars.
* Added default layer colors for Weave Map.
* Added COLOR-SCHEME support for `target=weave_map`.
* Improved ER Diagram readability in dark mode.

## Validation

* `/usr/bin/node --test test/*.test.mjs`
* `/usr/bin/node ./node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck`
* `/usr/bin/node esbuild.config.mjs production`
* `git diff --check`
releases.name: Release 0.1.14

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.13

This release expands Domain-based modeling across process and DFD views, improves documentation and templates, and polishes English/Japanese UI labels.

### Highlights

* Added Domain Sources support for `app_process` and `dfd_diagram`
* Added `Steps.domain` support for app_process domain placement
* Added local `## Domains` and external `## Domain Sources` resolution for app_process
* Added Domain hierarchy rendering and Domain group color scheme support for app_process and DFD
* Aligned Domain Sources table format across supported model types
* Normalized `domains` / `domain_diagram` render modes and settings labels
* Polished templates, README, and format documentation
* Improved English/Japanese UI localization consistency across preview, diagnostics, and related views
* Fixed Screen preview clipping for empty sections

### Verification

* `/usr/bin/node --test test/*.test.mjs`
* `/usr/bin/node ./node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck`
* `/usr/bin/node esbuild.config.mjs production`
* `git diff --check`

See `docs/releases/v0.1.13.md` for detailed release notes.
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.13

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
```md
## Model Weave 0.1.12

This release adds Domain modeling and Color Scheme support for richer business and system visualization.

### Added

- Added standalone `domains` model files for reusable Domain definitions.
- Added `domain_diagram` for integrating multiple Domains files into Mindmap, Area, and Tree views.
- Added `color_scheme` model files and global Color Scheme setting.
- Added DFD-local `## Domains` and `Objects.domain` grouping.
- Added Color Scheme support for Domains, Domain Diagram, DFD, and app_process Business Flow views.
- Added Applied Color Scheme display as a visible `kind` / color dictionary.
- Added PNG export support for Domains and Domain Diagram views.
- Added Domains, Domain Diagram, and Color Scheme template insertion commands.

### Documentation and samples

- Added format docs for Domains, Domain Diagram, and Color Scheme.
- Updated DFD Diagram format docs for local Domains and object Domain grouping.
- Refreshed sample model vaults for the 0.1.12 showcase.
```
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.12

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.11

Maintenance release focused on compatibility, diagnostics, and preview display quality.

### Fixes

- Fixed app_process Mermaid labels so escaped display characters and visible punctuation are handled more safely.
- Added shared display text decoding for app_process labels.
- Fixed data_object parsing so `## Source Links` is treated as a boundary after `## Fields`.
- Removed active `display: contents` usage for better Obsidian compatibility.
- Improved Japanese localization coverage for user-facing diagnostics and warning messages.
- Removed repetitive supported-type preview description text.
- Fixed compact Screen target cards so link text is not clipped.

### Notes

- ZenUML is not used because Obsidian's built-in Mermaid renderer does not currently support it.
- Broader Obsidian lint / CSS compatibility checks are deferred to a future maintenance task.
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.11

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.10

### Fixed

- Fixed lower pane section render order.

### Documentation

- Aligned DFD diagram format documentation with the current parser schema.
- Aligned Mapping format documentation with the current parser schema.
- Added a minimal schema-driven relations sample.
- Updated sample documentation.

### Release

- Updated release asset workflow to build release assets on GitHub Actions.
- Added artifact attestation generation for release assets.
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.10

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.9

This release improves Mermaid rendering and Business Flow modeling.

### Added

- Added Mermaid Detail view for Class / Class Diagram.
- Added Mermaid Detail view for Entity / ER Diagram.
- Added Mermaid Source panel for Mermaid-based renderers.
- Added configurable Mermaid Render Debug visibility.
- Added Business Flow node shapes based on `Steps.kind`.
- Added implicit Business Flow generation from `Steps` row order.
- Added merged implicit / explicit flow behavior for `app_process`.
- Added GitHub Actions workflow to upload release assets.

### Improved

- Improved Business Flow readability and layout consistency.
- Improved `Flows.label` / `Flows.condition` display priority.
- Improved Wikilink display in Business Flow edge labels.
- Improved ER Mermaid Detail dark mode readability.
- Improved PNG export filenames by including the renderer name.
- Cleaned up render mode selection behavior.
- Updated render mode documentation from V0.7 to V0.8 policy.
- Updated English and Japanese `FORMAT-app_process` documentation.

### Changed

- `render_mode: auto` is no longer presented as the normal user-facing default mode.
- Initial render mode is based on:
  1. frontmatter `render_mode`
  2. format-specific default render mode setting
  3. safe fallback
- Toolbar renderer selection is treated as a temporary view-level change.

### Deferred

- Mermaid color grouping by `kind` / `area` / `layer`
- Cross-renderer color scheme settings
- Mermaid click navigation
- Sequence Diagram
- State Transition Diagram
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.9

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.8

Model Weave 0.1.8 is a public release that includes both the internal 0.1.7 Relationship View milestone and the new 0.1.8 Codeset / State Usage improvements.

The main focus of this release is to make Markdown-based models easier to inspect, trace, and maintain. It adds a relationship-oriented view for navigating between models and introduces structured codeset/state value usage tracking.

### Highlights

* Added Relationship View / Impact View for model navigation and impact analysis.
* Added inbound and outbound model relationship summaries.
* Added unresolved reference display.
* Added related Source Links grouping.
* Added copyable Relationship Summary.
* Added codeset value usage tracking inside Relationship View.
* Added support for `app_process` `Flows.condition` value usage detection.
* Added support for Screen structured condition fields.
* Updated V0.8 format documentation and command-generated templates.
* Added GitHub Actions build workflow.
* Added Codex Git operation policy.
* Fixed ERD resolved relations layout overlap.

---

## Included internal 0.1.7 milestone

The internal 0.1.7 milestone introduced the foundation for Relationship View / Impact View.

### Relationship View / Impact View

Relationship View helps inspect how a model is connected to other models.

It can show:

* Objects referenced by the current model.
* Objects that reference the current model.
* Unresolved references.
* Related Source Links.
* Copyable relationship summary in Markdown.
* Open buttons for related models.
* Collapsible relationship and source link groups.

This makes it easier to follow the path from design models to related models and implementation source links.

### Source Links integration

Related Source Links are now surfaced from relationship context where possible.

This is useful when a model is connected to implementation files, rules, mappings, screens, or processes, and users want to quickly understand the likely impact area.

---

## New in 0.1.8

### Codeset / State Usage

0.1.8 adds codeset value usage tracking into Relationship View.

When viewing a `codeset` model, Model Weave can now show where specific codeset values are used in structured fields.

For example:

```markdown
[[CODE-INVENTORY-STATUS]].available
[[CODE-INVENTORY-STATUS]].shortage
```

These value references can appear under `Value usage` in Relationship View.

### Supported structured usage locations

Codeset value usage is intentionally conservative and based on structured fields only.

Supported examples include:

* `app_process` `Flows.condition`
* Screen `Fields.condition`
* Screen `Actions.condition`
* Screen `Messages.condition`
* Screen Local Process `Steps.condition`
* Screen Local Process `Errors.condition`

Model Weave does not infer codeset usage from arbitrary prose in this release.

### V0.8 format updates

The format documentation and command-generated templates were updated for V0.8 structured condition usage.

Key points:

* Natural language text remains useful for human explanation.
* Structured table fields are used for analyzable relationships.
* Screen does not gain `Flows` in V0.8.
* Complex flows should be modeled as `app_process`.
* Screen uses `condition` columns for UI state, action execution, message display, and screen-local behavior.
* Notes and Summary text are not parsed for codeset/state usage.

### Screen format updates

Screen templates now include structured condition fields such as:

* `Fields.condition`
* `Actions.condition`
* `Messages.condition`
* Local Process `Steps.condition`
* Local Process `Errors.condition`

Screen Local Processes are intended for lightweight screen-local behavior such as clear/reset, initial display, validation, display control, button enable/disable control, and message control.

Complex processing flows should still be modeled as `app_process`.

---

## Fixed

* Fixed ERD resolved relations layout overlap with Relationship View.
* Improved Relationship View value usage display so value usage is grouped by codeset value instead of compressed into long inline rows.

---

## Development / maintenance

* Added GitHub Actions build workflow.
* Added Codex Git operation policy to prevent unintended remote Git operations such as push, tag push, release creation, or merging into `develop` / `main`.

---

## Notes

* `0.1.7` was an internal milestone and was not published as a separate public release.
* `0.1.8` includes both the internal 0.1.7 Relationship View work and the V0.8 Codeset / State Usage work.
* This release does not include Mermaid renderer expansion. Mermaid renderer improvements are planned separately.

---

## Out of scope for this release

The following are not included in 0.1.8:

* Natural-language reference extraction from Notes or Summary.
* State transition diagrams.
* Codeset value usage heatmaps.
* Screen Flow support.
* Mermaid renderer expansion.
* Separate Codeset Usage View.
* Automatic value detection from labels or value codes without a codeset identifier.
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.8

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.6

This release adds an experimental app_process Business Flow preview and improves chart readability and fitting behavior.

### Highlights
- Added experimental app_process Business Flow preview using table-based `Steps` and `Flows`.
- Added lane-based Mermaid rendering for Business Flow.
- Added Business Flow documentation, template, and sample.
- Improved Mermaid chart readability in dark mode.
- Aligned Screen custom preview palette with other chart views.
- Improved Class custom preview Fit behavior when method text wraps.

### Business Flow
- `Steps` can be written as a Markdown table for structured process steps.
- `Flows` can define step-to-step edges, including branching.
- `lane` groups steps into Mermaid subgraphs.
- Steps without `lane` are rendered outside lane groups.
- `kind: flow` / `subflow` can represent a child flow or another app_process.

### Fixes and Improvements
- Business Flow now renders in the upper split-pane chart area.
- PNG export supports Business Flow diagrams.
- Mermaid charts are more readable in dark mode.
- Class custom preview Fit now accounts better for wrapped method text.
- Preview/detail styling has been made more consistent.

### Notes
Business Flow is experimental in this release. Existing prose-style app_process `Steps` remain compatible and continue to render as text.
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.6

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.5

This release improves Screen previews and fixes duplicate model ID diagnostics.

### Highlights
- Enhanced single Screen preview layout for screen specifications.
- Added transition target status display for Screen actions.
- Added scoped CSS classes for Screen preview customization with Obsidian CSS snippets.
- Fixed duplicate model ID diagnostics after shallow startup indexing.

### Added
- Added clearer Screen preview sections:
  - Screen Overview
  - Structure / Layout
  - UI Elements / Fields
  - Behavior / Actions
  - Transition target status
  - Transitions / Outgoing screens
  - Messages
- Added Screen Preview CSS customization documentation in English and Japanese.

### Improved
- Improved Screen preview table readability.
- Improved Screen chart and detail CSS scoping so chart areas and detail tables can be customized separately.
- Improved Source Links / localSourceRoot documentation.
- Added notes that Source Links may resolve or open local files outside the vault when `localSourceRoot` is configured.

### Fixed
- Fixed duplicate model ID diagnostics after shallow startup indexing.
- Duplicate model ID warnings are now recomputed globally and shown on every file participating in the duplicate.
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.5

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.4

### Improved
- Improved startup performance with lightweight startup indexing and lazy parsing.
- Deferred member diagnostics for shallow models to avoid false unresolved-member warnings.
- Updated README / README_JP with Source Links, localSourceRoot, and performance notes.

### Fixed
- Reduced unnecessary startup work for larger model vaults.
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.4

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Model Weave 0.1.3

This release improves preview usability and adds Source Links support.

### Added
- Added `## Source Links` support for model files.
- Added Local source root setting to resolve relative Source Links to external source repositories.
- Added Source Links preview columns: Path, Status, Resolved Path, Notes, and Actions.
- Added Copy Path and Open actions for resolved source paths.
- Added common sections documentation for Source Links.

### Improved
- Improved Fit positioning for single-object previews.
- Improved lower Connection details layout behavior.
- Improved text selection usability in detail, warning, error, and diagnostics areas.

### Fixed
- Empty Source Links path rows are ignored to avoid preview layout issues.
- Source path handling now supports Windows, WSL/UNC, and POSIX-style paths more safely.
releases.name: Model Weave 0.1.3

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## 0.1.2

Maintenance release after Community Plugin publication.

### Changed

- Updated README / READMEJP to reflect Community Plugins availability.
- Added Known Limitations section.
- Updated release version to 0.1.2.

### Notes

- No feature behavior changes.
- No renderer changes.

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
## Changes

- Fixed Obsidian Community Portal review issues.
- Updated `minAppVersion` to `1.7.2`.
- Replaced direct inline style usage with CSS classes and CSS variables.
- Improved Mermaid rendering safety.
- Updated release assets for Community Plugin review.

releases.author: nejimakibird
releases.description:
Initial public release of Model Weave.

- Text-first modeling in Markdown
- Relationship and diagram previews
- Diagnostics and PNG export
